channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Sony Reveals $766m Bungie Writedown in Latest Financial Results
item.content:encoded:
Sony has released its financial results for the 2025 fiscal year, and the publisher has reported a pretty big write-down on the part of Marathon and Destiny developer Bungie.

As revealed in Sony's latest financial report, the Japanese gaming giant recorded "impairment losses" to the tune of about 120 billion yen on Bungie's part. That works out at around $766 million, so it's not an insignificant loss.

The loss is perhaps a little worrying given that Sony's financial year ends on March 31st, which means that it takes into account the release of extraction shooter Marathon. Admittedly, the game belongs to a niche genre that was probably never going to carry Sony's live-service ambitions single-handedly, but this will almost certainly be making executives nervous.

Despite the loss suffered as a result of Bungie in 2025, Sony says it intends to continue supporting Marathon by "working to retain highly engaged core users". The publisher believes that Marathon's "engagement metrics such as retention...remain at a high level".

In general financial terms, Sony's gaming division's sales have remained "essentially flat" when compared to the 2024 fiscal year, while operating income has seen a 12% increase due to "sales from network services" and the positive impact of foreign currency exchange rates.

However, Sony is forecasting a drop in sales revenue for the 2026 financial year due to a projected decrease in PlayStation 5 and other hardware sales, although that will be offset by a predicted increase in the sale of first-party games.

The publisher points to releases like PS5 exclusives Saros and Marvel's Wolverine as reasons to be optimistic about sales for the coming year. Notably, both of these games are single-player titles, while Bungie's efforts have exclusively been in the live-service space for over a decade.

If you'd like to catch up with Sony's latest financials, you can check out the latest earnings report here, and you can read a summary of the company's thoughts on where its financials stand right here.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
